qas.q: What T.O. do you use to determine serviceability for a vehicle or piece of equipment?
If a manufacturers service manual has a different serviceability standard than the T.O. 36-1-191, which one will have precedence?
qas.a: Manufacturers service manual.

qas.q: What is involved in a complete serviceability inspection?
qas.a: Visually inspecting and functionally testing all of the components on a vehicle in accordance with T.O. 36-1-191.

qas.q: What are some of the factors to consider when applying serviceable standards?
qas.a:
-Age and mileage of the vehicle
-Purpose of the vehicle and the job it performs
-Remaining service life
-Relative value of services the vehicle returns in comparison with the cost of renovation, replacement, or parts
-One time repair limit

qas.q: If consistently applied, what is the advantage of serviceability standards?
qas.a: Can save hours and vehicle down time and thousands of dollars in unnecessary parts replacement.

qas.q: What type of inspection is required on a daily and weekly basis?
qas.a: Operator inspection.

qas.q: What form is used by vehicle operators as guides in their inspection and recording of vehicle discrepancies?
qas.a: AF Form 1800

qas.q: What is the role of the VCO and VCNCO?
qas.a: They act as liaison between their unit and the vehicle management flight.

qas.q: List the types of safety deficiencies that must be reported to vehicle management.
qas.a:
-Tires or brakes
-Steering mechanisms
-Operating levers (controlling power transmissions, hoisting, dumping, and tripping devices)
-Warning lights (turn signals, brake lights, emergency flashers or rotating flashers)
-Headlights, reflectors, and clearance lights
-Windshield wipers or defrosters
-Other similar and warning equipment and devices on unique to special purpose units
-Any other condition reasonably considered to be a safety hazard

qas.q: Who develops a long-range plan to balance shop work loads?
qas.a: VM&A.

qas.q: What factors may justify adjusting preventative maintenance intervals?
qas.a:
-Operational environment
-Operational utilization
-Operational speed
-Intervals that enhance safety and continued operation of the vehicle

qas.q: What is the definition of PM&I?
qas.a: Periodic prescribed inspection, or servicing of equipment, accomplished on a calendar date, mileage, or hours-of-operation basis.

qas.q: What checklist do technicians use when performing PM&I?
qas.a: AF Form 4354.

qas.q: List examples of abnormalities that may be found during a PM&I
qas.a:
-Burnt transmission fluid
-Inoperative instrument panel gauges
-Bearing noises

qas.q: What is the function of a lube chart?
qas.a: To show the technician all the specific lubrication points on the vehicle.

qas.q: Where should lube charts be maintained in vehicle management?
qas.a: In the lubrication area.

qas.q: How often is the vehicle condition inspection conducted?
qas.a: Every time a vehicle enters the shop, or every 12 months, whichever comes first.

qas.q: What form is used to document the vehicle condition inspection?
qas.a: AF Form 4355.

qas.q: What is the primary purpose of the special inspection?
qas.a: To increase safety and operational reliability.

qas.q: How often are wheel bearings packed?
qas.a: Every 3 years; 36,000 miles; 59,700 kilometers, or 2,400 hours on all vehicles.

qas.q: What is used to document the technical inspection?
qas.a: AF Form 91

qas.q: While performing a technical inspection on a crash fire truck, you complete blocks 1 through 63 of the AFTO Form 91. What additional blocks, if any, need to be completed?
qas.a: 65 through 110

qas.q: What is the objective of the Hazardous Waste Management Program?
qas.a: To ensure hazardous waste is handles in a manner that protects human health and the environment.

qas.q: What is the meaning of "cradle-to-grave" in regards to hazardous waste?
qas.a: From waste generation to disposal.

qas.q: What is our basic duty in complying with the Hazardous Waste Program?
qas.a: Ensure the waste we produce is collected, fully documented, identified, and transported to an approve treatment, storage, or disposal facility.

qas.q: What can we do to reduce the strain on landfills?
qas.a: Reduce consumption and recycle.

qas.q: What category does the majority of automotive hazardous wastes fall?
qas.a: F - Nonspecific sources.

qas.q: What should vehicle management do with empty containers of hazardous materials?
qas.a: They must be turned in to hazardous waste accumulation or satellite accumulation point for disposal.

qas.q: What is an MSDS?
qas.a: The primary link in a communication process between manufacturer and the handler, or user of the material.

qas.q: List 4 situations when you should refer to the MSDS.
qas.a:
-Before you handle the material for the first time.
-If you have questions about the materials physical characteristics.
-If you have questions about how or where to store the material.
-When you have to clean up a spill or a leak and dispose of the material.

qas.q: What factors listed in the MSDS help when decided what management practices are needed in the work area?
qas.a:
-Toxicity
-Reactivity
-Flammability
-First Aid
-Personnel Protection
-Spill or leak procedures
-Physical identification
-Product identification

qas.q: What is specific gravity?
qas.a: The measure of a materials density relative to that of water.

qas.q: Who is considered a hazardous waste generator?
qas.a: A facility owner, operator, or person who first produced the hazardous waste.

qas.q: What is one of the keys to avoiding liability?
qas.a: A good and accurate record.

qas.q: State 2 reasons why you shouldn't mix different hazardous wastes.
qas.a:
-Mixing can cause violent and dangerous chemical reactions
-Mixed contaminants are very expensive to dispose of

qas.q: Why should hazardous waste containers be placed on pallets?
qas.a: To reduce corrosion caused by contact with the ground.

qas.q: What is defined as a satellite accumulation point?
qas.a: The practice of allowing the collection of small amounts of hazardous waste at or near the point of generation.

qas.q: What punishment is rendered for violating environmental laws?
qas.a: Prison terms of 6 months to 15 years, and one-time fines anywhere from $10,000 to $150,000, with cumulative fines as high as $50,000 per day for violation.

qas.q: What is waste minimization?
qas.a: Source reduction or recycling action reducing the total volume or quantity of hazardous waste.

qas.q: State 3 reasons to minimize the generation of hazardous waste.
qas.a:
-It saves the environment
-Its the law
-Its AF policy

qas.q: State 3 ways that may help in minimizing waste generation.
qas.a:
-Ensure shop operating procedures address waste minimization
-Conduct an employee training and awareness program
-Conduct proper inventory control

qas.q: State 3 ways you can help reduce water pollution.
qas.a:
-Recycle used vehicle oil
-Do not dump household cleaning products down the drain or on the ground
-Minimize the use of pesticides and fertilizers on you lawn

qas.q: What is the key to reducing energy and air pollution?
qas.a: Energy conservation.

qas.q: What organization has overall responsibility for the corrosion program?
qas.a: Vehicle Managment

qas.q: What are the 4 levels of corrosion control protection?
qas.a:
-Type A: Factory
-Type B: Mild rust proofing
-Type C: Design corrosion control
-Type D: Tropical corrosion control

qas.q: What T.O. provides procedures for corrosion control?
When is an initial corrosion inspection accomplished?
qas.a: During the acceptance inspection.

qas.q: What is the purpose of the corrosion control program?
qas.a: To ensure vehicles and equipment items are maintained in a condition that prevents significant vehicle deterioration.

qas.q: What are the levels of vehicle preservation; briefly describe each.
qas.a:
-Level A: Protection over 90 days
-Level B: Protection for approximately 90 days
-Level C: Protection for less then 90 days
-Live storage: Requires immediate operational readiness; minimal protection

qas.q: What T.O. provides instructions for storage of Air Force vehicles?
What is a very important consideration when storing vehicles?
qas.a: The storage site.

qas.q: How often should vehicles in Level A storage receive a visual inspection?
qas.a: Every 90 days.

qas.q: What form do you use to perform a serviceability inspection when the vehicle is in storage?
qas.a: AF Form 91

qas.q: How often are WRM vehicles exercised?
qas.a: Every 30 days, save those stored in plastic bags.

qas.q: What is your first step in preparing a vehicle for shipment?
qas.a: Perform an LTI to determine vehicle condition.

qas.q: Who certifies the LTI form to ensure all entries are corrected and the vehicle meets all serviceability standards for shipment?
qas.a: VFM/VMS

qas.q: How much of the vehicle's anticipated life must remain before shipment overseas?
qas.a: At least 75%.

qas.q: What requirements must vehicles shipped under the MAP meet?
qas.a:
-Vehicle appearance should be above average
-Vehicle must have all components, assemblies, and parts to be considered complete
-All TCTOs are completed.
-Vehicle components must have at least 50% of anticipated life remaining
